title: fagara ruiziana
intro: Fagara ruiziana, also known as the Japanese prickly ash, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties, which are attributed to its active compounds, including limonoids and alkaloids. The medicinal properties of Fagara ruiziana have been found to provide health benefits such as pain relief, fever reduction, and improved cardiovascular health. Fagara ruiziana is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various conditions.
